summary: PR #2613 split Kirby copied-special code out of `ftkirbyyoshiegg` into separate Mars, Peach, and Game & Watch translation units, added their headers, wired includes and build configuration, adjusted GALE01 split ranges, and changed several symbol metadata entries from global/generated names to local labels. The PR body explicitly says Mars was also split. No review or issue comments were present in the slice.

key_files.role: Split metadata updated: `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` text/sdata2 range narrowed and new ranges added for `ftkirbyspecialmars.c`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c`; one `itmewtwoshadowball.c` data range adjusted.
Symbol metadata adjusted, including local labels for data/string/float entries around item and Kirby copied-special data.
Build object list updated to add `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` as NonMatching and `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c`/`ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` as Matching.
Kirby include wiring changed from `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` toward the newly split copied-special headers.
Former larger source file reduced after Mars, Peach, and Game & Watch copied-special code was split out.
Old header content removed as declarations moved to new split headers.
New split Mars copied-special source file added.
New header for Mars copied-special declarations.
New split Peach copied-special source file added and listed as Matching in configure.py.
New header for Peach copied-special declarations.
New split Game & Watch copied-special source file added and listed as Matching in configure.py; excerpt shows Chef-related callbacks, motion-state transitions, item calls, and PAD_STACK use.
New header for Game & Watch copied-special declarations.
Item source touched as part of Peach copied-special split/link work.
Item source touched as part of Game & Watch copied-special split/link work.

what_changed: The Kirby Yoshiegg split range was shortened from text `0x8010AA64-0x8010D740` to `0x8010AA64-0x8010B1F4`, with new `.sdata` and reduced `.sdata2` ranges.
New split entries were added for `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` (`0x8010B1F4-0x8010C288`), `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` (`0x8010C288-0x8010CE5C`), and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` (`0x8010CE5C-0x8010D740`).
Configure object registration added `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` as `NonMatching` and `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` plus `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` as `Matching`.
New C and header files were added for Mars, Peach, and Game & Watch Kirby copied specials, while `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` was deleted and `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` had a large deletion count.
`ftkirby.c` include wiring changed to include the new split headers and stop including `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h`.
Several GALE01 symbol entries were changed from global `it_*`, `ftKb_Init_*`, or `MSL_Math_*` style names to local labels such as `@237`, `@188`, and `...data.0`.
`itpeachtoad.c` and `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c` had small supporting changes, but the provided excerpt does not show exact code details for those files.

smart_moves.text: The split was reflected consistently across split metadata, build configuration, source files, headers, and include wiring rather than only adding new C files.
The PR separated the newly linked copied-special code by character/source responsibility: Mars, Peach, and Game & Watch each received its own translation unit and header.

Symbol metadata was adjusted alongside split changes, including scope/local-label changes around affected data ranges, instead of compensating in C with invented global storage in the visible excerpt.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a previously combined fighter translation unit, update all boundary surfaces together: GALE01 splits, configure object list, owning C files, headers, and include sites.
Split metadata may require not only text range subdivision but also associated `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and symbol-scope updates for constants and local labels owned by the newly separated units.
Configure status can differ among files in the same split batch; this PR added Mars as `NonMatching` while adding Peach and Game & Watch as `Matching`.

naming_conventions.text: New Kirby copied-special files use the lowercase `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c/.h` path pattern, as shown by `ftkirbyspecialmars`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch`.

Public function names in the visible Game & Watch source use `ftKb_SpecialNGw_*` and callback role suffixes such as `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`; unresolved/private helpers remain address-style `fn_8010CE5C` and `fn_8010CFB0`.

Recovered local roles in the visible Game & Watch code use short project-style locals such as `fp`, `da`, `gobj`, `pos`, and `vec0`/`vec1`, while less certain names like `var_r28` and `temp_r5` remain generated-looking.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The visible Game & Watch source uses `PAD_STACK` in several functions, including around sausage-selection logic and animation/IASA handlers. The slice does not include build or objdiff evidence justifying these stack tactics.

A natural `for` loop is visible in the Game & Watch sausage-selection code (`for (i = 5, i -= 5; i < 5; i++)`) rather than fully duplicated blocks, though the unusual initializer appears codegen-oriented.

Symbol metadata changes convert several generated/global-looking data symbols to local labels at specific addresses, suggesting split/link matching work was done at the metadata layer rather than through fake C data anchors in the shown excerpt.

follow_up_queries: ftkirbyyoshiegg split history ftkirbyspecialmars ftkirbyspecialpeach ftkirbyspecialgamewatch
itpeachtoad changes related to ftkirbyspecialpeach split
itkirbygamewatchchefpan changes related to ftkirbyspecialgamewatch split
GALE01 symbols local labels ftKb_Init_804D9580 PR 2613

The raw diff is truncated in the loaded slice, so exact contents of all new/modified C and header files are not fully visible.
No review comments, issue comments, or review records are present, so reviewer intent and review feedback cannot be inferred.
The configure excerpt labels Peach and Game & Watch objects as `Matching` and Mars as `NonMatching`, but no build, objdiff, or regression logs are included in the PR slice.
Item-source changes are known from metadata but not detailed in the visible excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU splits require synchronized split metadata, configure registration, headers, and includes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2613 shows a Kirby copied-special split landing through coordinated updates to `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, `configure.py`, new split C/header files, and `ftkirby.c` include wiring.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split/link PRs may need symbol-scope metadata fixes
accepted_candidate_records.text: The same split PR changed several `symbols.txt` entries from global/generated names to local labels near affected data ranges, making symbol metadata part of the split/link surface.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby copied-special split path pattern
source_update_candidates.text: Kirby copied-special implementations in this PR are placed under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/` with lowercase names of the form `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c/.h`, including `ftkirbyspecialmars`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path/naming fact, but should be curator-reviewed against current tree and broader naming history before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: PR #2613 split ranges for Kirby copied specials
source_update_candidates.text: The PR slice shows `ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` narrowed to text `0x8010AA64-0x8010B1F4`, with new split ranges: `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` `0x8010B1F4-0x8010C288`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` `0x8010C288-0x8010CE5C`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` `0x8010CE5C-0x8010D740`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Exact split-range data is source-owned metadata and should be verified against current `splits.txt` before any source-corpus update.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat PAD_STACK in split/link PRs as an evidence-required tactic
source_update_candidates.text: The visible `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` code includes multiple `PAD_STACK` uses, but the slice contains no objdiff/build evidence. Existing standards already require explicit evidence for PAD_STACK and similar tactics; this PR can serve as an example candidate only if curator wants examples attached to that standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Not a new standard; only a possible example for the existing evidence requirement.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that Peach and Game & Watch were objectively verified by objdiff solely from `Object(Matching, ...)`; the PR slice lacks validation output.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or feedback because there are no review comments, issue comments, or reviews in the slice.
Do not promote the observed `PAD_STACK` uses as accepted permanent style; current standards require explicit verifier evidence, which is absent here.
Do not generalize the item-source changes beyond the file paths touched; the visible excerpt does not show the exact `itpeachtoad.c` or `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c` edits.
